properties.name: audit.logging.enabled
properties.description: Globally enables the audit logging library. When disabled, the auto-configured interceptors, filters, listeners, storage beans, and internal endpoint stay inactive.

properties.name: audit.logging.async.rejection-policy
properties.description: Policy used when the async audit executor queue is full. CALLER_RUNS protects audit data at the cost of caller latency; DISCARD and DISCARD_OLDEST favor latency; ABORT raises a rejection exception.

properties.name: audit.logging.capture.max-body-size
properties.description: Maximum request or response body size, in bytes, copied into an audit record. Larger bodies are replaced with a clear truncation marker.

properties.name: audit.logging.capture.max-header-size
properties.description: Maximum serialized request or response header size, in bytes, copied into an audit record. Headers are redacted before this limit is applied.

properties.name: audit.logging.capture.included-paths
properties.description: Ant-style inbound request path patterns allowed for audit capture. Defaults to all paths so existing applications keep the same behavior.

properties.name: audit.logging.capture.excluded-paths
properties.description: Ant-style inbound request path patterns excluded from audit capture. Exclusions win over included paths and are useful for health checks, static assets, or endpoints that should never be copied into audit records.

properties.name: audit.logging.storage.type
properties.description: Preferred built-in storage implementation when more than one storage module is present. Supported values are jpa, jdbc, memory, and kafka. Direct storage modules require this value explicitly; the starter supplies jpa as its convenience default.

properties.name: audit.logging.flyway.enabled
properties.description: Adds the audit library's vendor-specific Flyway migration location to the host application's Flyway configuration.

properties.name: audit.logging.kafka.enabled
properties.description: Enables Kafka as the audit sink when the Kafka storage module is present. Kafka is a streaming sink and does not provide internal search by itself.

properties.name: audit.logging.kafka.topic
properties.description: Kafka topic used by the built-in Kafka audit sink.

properties.name: audit.logging.internal.api-key
properties.description: API key required in the X-Audit-Api-Key header for /internal/audit-logs. Blank or missing values block the endpoint fail-secure.

properties.name: audit.logging.masking.additional-fields
properties.description: Additional JSON field names to redact beyond the built-in sensitive fields. Matching is case-insensitive.

values.description: Use JPA entity/repository based persistence and search.
Use JdbcTemplate based persistence and search without JPA.
Use in-process storage for demos and tests.
Publish audit records to Kafka as a write-only stream sink.
